title: Xtra Crunchy Ethiopian Lentil Casserole
description: A herbal and crunchy Ethiopian dish featuring pancetta and en-croute, perfect for a low-calorie friendly meal during the monsoon season.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot, bring 2 cups of water to a boil and add the lentils, cooking until they are tender.
In a large skillet, cook the pancetta over medium heat until crispy, then set aside.
In the same skillet, add the onion, garlic, carrots, and zucchini, cooking until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the tomato paste, berbere spice, and cooked lentils, cooking for 2 minutes.
Roll out the puff pastry and place the lentil mixture in the center, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges of the pastry with olive oil and fold the pastry over the filling, pressing the edges to seal.
Place the casserole on a baking sheet and bake for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before serving.
